articles.title: Shaquille O’Neal Confident He Could Beat Victor Wembanyama One‑on‑One
articles.original_title: Shaquille O’Neal Confident In One-On-One Matchup Vs Victor Wembanyama
articles.summary: Shaquille O’Neal believes he could hold his own against Spurs rookie Victor Wembanyama in a head‑to‑head matchup. He noted that while Wembanyama has already taken San Antonio to the NBA Finals in just his third season, the series against the New York Knicks (down 3‑1) will serve as a valuable learning experience for the young star.
key_points: O’Neal publicly expressed confidence in his ability to compete with Wembanyama.
Wembanyama’s Spurs reached the Finals in his first postseason, now trailing the Knicks 3‑1.
The series is viewed as a developmental milestone for Wembanyama’s future as a league face.

articles.title: Danny Green Explains Types of Players Lakers Need Around Luka Dončić
articles.original_title: Danny Green Outlines Type Of Players Lakers Need To Surround Luka Doncic
articles.summary: In a Lakers Nation piece, veteran sharpshooter Danny Green outlines the roster pieces the Los Angeles Lakers should target in free agency to complement star guard Luka Dončić. He stresses the importance of adding shooters, defenders, and complementary scorers to avoid over‑reliance on Dončić and Austin Reaves.
key_points: Free agency begins June 30, and the Lakers must add multiple role players to become a true contender.
Green highlights the need for three‑point specialists and versatile defenders.
Reducing dependence on Dončić and Reaves will create a more balanced offensive attack.

articles.title: Boston Exploits Luka Dončić in Pick‑and‑Roll, Targets Austin Reaves
articles.original_title: Magic: “Boston looked up and said, ‘Okay, I’m going to put Luka in the pick-and-roll every time.’ Then Tatum had him saying, ‘Oh, you want me? Okay, I’m just going to go past you or pull up for my jump shot,’ because he knew he could beat him off the dribble. They did the same thing to Austin Reaves
articles.summary: Boston Celtics adjusted their defense to force Luka Dončić into pick‑and‑roll situations, where Jayson Tatum would either drive past him or pull up for a jump shot, exploiting Dončić’s difficulty defending off the dribble. The Celtics employed a similar strategy against Austin Reaves, limiting his effectiveness in the game.
key_points: Celtics deliberately put Dončić in pick‑and‑rolls to create mismatches.
Jayson Tatum used his dribble‑penetration and shooting to neutralize Dončić.
Austin Reaves faced the same defensive scheme, reducing his impact.
